_note: Red-flag taxonomy used by the risk extraction engine. Each category defines the human label, default owning workstream, and signal phrases the (mock/live) scanner looks for in filings and data-room text.

categories.label: Customer concentration
categories.description: Revenue dependence on a small number of customers.

categories.label: Supplier / vendor concentration
categories.description: Dependence on key suppliers, vendors, or single cloud/infrastructure providers.

categories.label: Demand weakness
categories.description: Slowing growth, softening bookings, lengthening sales cycles, pipeline weakness.

categories.label: Margin pressure
categories.description: Rising costs, pricing pressure, unfavorable mix shift, compute/hosting cost inflation.

categories.label: Debt & liquidity
categories.description: Covenant pressure, refinancing risk, cash runway, working-capital strain.

categories.label: Legal & regulatory
categories.description: Litigation, investigations, compliance exposure, regulatory-regime dependence.

categories.label: Cyber & data security
categories.description: Breach history, security posture gaps, data-handling risk.

categories.label: Integration & M&A
categories.description: Acquisition integration risk, platform roll-up complexity, tech debt from prior deals.

categories.label: AI / technology disruption
categories.description: Automation/AI threat to the moat, platform dependency, model/vendor lock-in.

categories.label: GovCon / contract risk
categories.description: Federal contract concentration, recompete risk, agency budget exposure (extension).
